comments.body:
Hi @onlyjob. Thanks for your PR.

I'm waiting for a [containers](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) member to verify that this patch is reasonable to test. If it is, they should reply with `/ok-to-test` on its own line. Until that is done, I will not automatically test new commits in this PR, but the usual testing commands by org members will still work. Regular contributors should [join the org](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) to skip this step.

Once the patch is verified, the new status will be reflected by the `ok-to-test` label.

I understand the commands that are listed [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod).

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>
@onlyjob Thanks for the PR.  Our CI requires that all PR's get signed and I don't think you signed this one.  Can you please 'git commit --amend -s' and then repush the PR?

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *onlyjob*, *TomSweeneyRedHat* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [TomSweeneyRedHat] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
WOWSA!  Really nice change @onlyjob , thanks for all the corrections.  LGTM once the gating issue (signature me thinks) is solved.

/ok-to-test
Thanks @onlyjob, you need to sign your commit.
Was this a tool that you ran to generate this?
Would it be worth adding tests to make sure PRs do not contain additional typos?

Signed, thanks.
I've used "codespell" utility (e.g. `codespell -i1 -w`) installed by corresponding Debian package.
Incorporating spell check to CI may be a nice idea but _codespell_ is not great for automatic checking - it is more of a manual correction tool...

@onlyjob thanks for the info on codespell, that's a tool I'm unfamiliar with, but will definitely look it up.

This PR made it through the gating test, so we're over the signature hurdle.  A remote_client test failed, but I think that's an unrelated hiccup.  I've restarted the test and hopefully that will clear it up.
